title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in North Carolina
description: North Carolina’s 2026 U.S. Senate race is for one of the state’s two U.S. Senate seats. It is an open-seat contest because incumbent Republican Sen. Thom Tillis said he would not seek re-election, making the race a major partisan battleground with potential implications for control of the U.S. Senate. The race is widely viewed as competitive: North Carolina has been closely divided in recent presidential and statewide elections, and the matchup features former Democratic Gov. Roy Cooper against Republican former RNC chair Michael Whatley.

candidates.name: Roy Cooper
candidates.summary: Roy Cooper is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in North Carolina and previously served as the state’s governor from 2017 to 2025. His campaign emphasizes his record on public education, health care expansion, job growth, and bipartisan governing in a closely divided state.

summary_sources.title: Meet Roy | Roy Cooper for North Carolina

candidates.name: Michael Whatley
candidates.summary: Michael Whatley is the Republican nominee for U.S. Senate in North Carolina and previously served as chair of the Republican National Committee. His campaign presents him as a conservative aligned with Donald Trump, with a focus on lower costs, safe communities, and economic growth.
